qa_pairs.question: Who automatically has access to a RAC award on a general-purpose cluster or Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: By default, every role sponsored by the PI through their Alliance CCDB registration has access to the RAC award. Any co-PIs listed on the RAC application will also have access.

qa_pairs.question: How can a Principal Investigator (PI) select specific users for their allocation?
qa_pairs.answer: To select which users may use an allocation, a PI needs to log in to https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/, then from the 'My Account' menu, select 'Manage RAP Memberships'. On the subsequent page, they should select the desired Resource Allocation Project (RAP) from the drop-down list.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step to manage RAP memberships for an allocation?
qa_pairs.answer: The first step is to log in at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/.

qa_pairs.question: Where can a PI manage their RAP memberships?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI can manage their RAP memberships by logging into the CCDB portal at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/ and navigating to the 'Manage RAP Memberships' section under the 'My Account' menu. The direct URL for this page is https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/resource_allocation_projects/members.

qa_pairs.question: What menu option should I choose to manage members of a Resource Allocation Project (RAP)?
qa_pairs.answer: From the 'My Account' menu, you should select 'Manage RAP Memberships'.

qa_pairs.question: After navigating to the 'Manage RAP Memberships' page, what is the next step?
qa_pairs.answer: On the 'Manage RAP Memberships' page, you need to select the Resource Allocation Project (RAP) to which you want to add members from the drop-down list.
